title: Content Codings, Character Sets, Media Admission, and Digests · Runtime Contract: Media Admission
summary: Compressed and decoded sizes are conflated, unknown encodings are guessed, binary responses reach HTML parsing, and one digest represents every stage. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go media admission increment in representation decoding and identity ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Use transport media type plus conservative sniff evidence to accept HTML or XML and reject binary and ambiguous bodies.
Validate content-coding order and classify unsupported, corrupt, partial, over-budget, and cancellation outcomes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Predict Media Admission · Theory Media Admission

theory case — A theory Go team handles case 5000 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile wire and decoded budgets, coding chains, media, charsets, partial bodies, and stage digests; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-media-admission-9s6qoj(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_media_admission_9s6qoj\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?representationCases < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?accountedCases < 14 \|\| accountedCases > representationCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rejectedCases < 5)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?partialRecords > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The decision applies Media Admission to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Media Admission.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using transport and media plus specific case evidence.
